title: Jonathan Bird's Blue World
subTitle: Rivers underneath Mexico's Yucatan peninsula; some venomous fish are dangerous to humans; Greenland shark.

title: Adaptable Animals
subTitle: Janice has her hands full with a rambunctious young husky who just can't keep still; a special goat who wears four braces brings along his own fan club.

title: Super Senses
subTitle: Certain animals have some crazy looking sense organs; every feature has a special purpose that has evolved to ensure the survival of the animal.

title: Super Senses
subTitle: Animals have sense organs with which they can recognize electromagnetic fields; they can feel when an earthquake is approaching and sharks can track their prey.

title: Super Senses
subTitle: Living beings have an internal biological clock, modeled on the famous circadian rhythm of night and day and running over a period of 24 hours; this rhythm is particularly evident in the cicada, lizards, bears and Arctic ground squirrels.

title: Super Senses
subTitle: The sea hosts an enormous quantity of species of fish, mammals, and crustaceans that have unique mechanisms to withstand pressure, low temperatures, and total darkness of the seabed.

title: World's Wildest City: Anchorage
subTitle: Anchorage, ice-bound nearly all the year, explodes with life in summer; Northern flora and fauna adapt to the brief season, accelerating their activities to maximize the short window of opportunity.

title: World's Wildest City: Anchorage
subTitle: Alaska stands as Earth's last true wilderness; Its 600 square miles of pristine habitat host diverse wildlife; Caribou herds roam plains, wolves stalk forests, and brown bears rule rivers in this abundant ecosystem.

title: World's Wildest City: Anchorage
subTitle: Anchorage faces record insecurity levels in the Arctic; Animals, not humans, commit crimes, vandalism, burglaries, and petty thefts; Wildlife engages in criminal acts, challenging the city's safety norms.

title: Monkey Thieves
subTitle: Bipin and Tito try their luck with the palace garden females.

title: Monkey Thieves
subTitle: Bipin walks Jaipur's streets alone after losing his only friend.

subTitle: The team hunts the elusive Nile monitor lizard.

title: Python Hunters
subTitle: The team heads to Louisiana to conduct research on invasive reptile species.

subTitle: The guys attempt to track down a massive breeding snake in hopes of putting a dent in the invasive snake population.

title: Python Hunters
subTitle: Cold weather in the Everglades leads the team to hunt invasive snakes in Puerto Rico.

title: Kalahari Meerkats
subTitle: Cleo's pup Ophelia is left behind on a foraging trip and becomes the victim of a goshawk, which leaves the rest of the family feeling insecure.

title: Animal Armoury
subTitle: Animals like the Arctic fox, green tree frog and penguin, benefit from colouring that matches or blends with their surroundings.

title: Animal Armoury
subTitle: Showcases how some animals and creatures, such as toads, moths and stone fish, use their poison glands to survive in the wild.

subTitle: Creatures from the continent of South America; a jaguar's jaws' ability to crush bone; the growth of a Galapagos tortoise.

title: Blue Realm
subTitle: Unusual training program for sharks using visual cues and signals.

title: Blue Realm
subTitle: A look at practices for seeing great white sharks up close, including baiting or chumming, which are being questioned and banned in many countries due to a dramatic increase in shark attacks.

title: Blue Realm
subTitle: Seals and sea lions are observed in their natural habitat.

title: Blue Realm
subTitle: Seals and sea lions are observed in their natural habitat.

title: Rhino Orphanage
subTitle: The Rhino Orphanage receives a call for help, a calf was stuck in mud for three days, abandoned and unable to walk; Karen rushes to a rhino that may need to be euthanized and is possibly pregnant.

title: Rhino Orphanage
subTitle: It's a bittersweet farewell as the team releases the rehabilitated baby rhinos into the wild.

title: Wild Iberia
subTitle: Life begins once again as the sun is heating all in the Iberian peninsula; as animals awaken with the flowers, the softness of the weather has everyone building and breeding.

title: Wild Iberia
subTitle: With the exception of the mountains and the north of Iberia, almost everywhere else is over 30 C and some places in the south are even reaching 50 C, bringing about a great thirst among the animals.

title: Wild Iberia
subTitle: Summer ended several days ago and Autumn is invading the whole of Iberia as cicadas and the grasshoppers have ceased to sing and the ants will soon be eating their corpses.

title: Vet Tales
subTitle: Willow's leg is lame and Stacey decides to amputate; Jack the Jack Russel has been kicked out of doggy school and Stacey tries to help and Mark changes Shadow the cat from a male into a female.

title: Killer Instincts
subTitle: Takes a look at the domains and realms of dangerous animals like the king cobra, jaguar and African hippopotamus.

title: Killer Instincts
subTitle: Examines the lives of the American alligator, python and moray eel, three of the animal kingdom's best lurkers.

title: Killer Instincts
subTitle: The filmmakers explore the lives of three dangerous big cats, the cheetah, the snow leopard and the regal tiger, and how they hunt down their prey.

title: Killer Instincts
subTitle: Examines the lives of the fox, lynx and harpy eagle, three majestic and cunning predators living in the wild.

title: Wild Ones
subTitle: An exciting look at nature, with close-ups of wild cats, snakes, crocodiles, scorpions and more, focusing on their methods of attack and defense.

title: Wild Ones
subTitle: Nature documentary series exploring the different ways that animals behave This episode looks at some of the cutest creatures.

title: Wild Ones
subTitle: Exploring the most intelligent of animals; it could be the socially complex chimpanzee, the erudite elephant, the fastidious bird or the inimitable octopus.

title: Wild Ones
subTitle: On the world's largest island thrives a breathtaking variety of animals, including the bizarre platypus, adorable koalas, bouncing kangaroos and dingoes that prowl.

title: Animals at Work
subTitle: Prathida the elephant drummer gets ready for a special performance, a cat-rescuing dog is honoured as Cat of the Year and flesh eating dermatologist fish work overtime at a day spa in Ireland.

title: Animals at Work
subTitle: Misty the owl prepares for his first ever public flight, a Hungarian sheepdog competes at the national championships and a cherished police horse celebrates his retirement at Liverpool's Lord Mayor's Parade.

title: Animal Society
subTitle: Explore the family dynamic of wildebeests, elephants, and giraffes as we take a trip around Africa.

title: Animal Society
subTitle: Explore some of the 129 species of ocean mammals from groups of cetaceans, sirenians, pinnipeds and fissipeds.

title: Animal Society
subTitle: Birds have colonized every inch of our planet, from the hottest jungles to the barren ice shelves of Antarctica; coming in all shapes and sizes, each has adapted to its environment with unique lifestyles.

title: Animal Society
subTitle: Caniforms inhabit various habitats worldwide; divided into nine families, including all bears, wolves and foxes, their size and lifestyles vary considerably.

title: Race of Life
subTitle: Animals adapt through a series of small and random mutations to compete more effectively for food, water and space. Certain creatures have gone through different processes in order to evolve into what they are today.

title: Race of Life
subTitle: Techniques big cats use to help them win the race of life.

title: Race of Life
subTitle: A look at how desert animals have evolved to withstand the extreme heat and lack of water, while exploring the history of scorpions.

title: Race of Life
subTitle: A look at how elephants survive on a strict plant-and-vegetable-based diet, as they explore the African Savannah and fight thousands of battles everyday.

title: Wild About
subTitle: Over a hundred people are killed every year by enraged elephants in India alone; though often seen as a symbol for calmness, patience and exotic nobility, these true giants of the wild will need protection in the years to come.

title: Wild About
subTitle: Once highly endangered but now flourishing thanks to a concentrated effort to preserve their natural habitat, crocodiles continue to stalk waters as one of the last remaining predators on earth.

title: Inside Animal A&E
subTitle: The team treats Labrador Scuby's lumps, nervous cat Wotzit's jabs, rescue collie Bob and tiny rat Squeaky's stomach growth in a day of unusual cases.

title: Inside Animal A&E
subTitle: Team faces a day of critical animal care from plus-size cat Bob to Angus's gum surgery, a Husky's sore paw and Bandit's urgent bladder operation.

title: Inside Animal A&E
subTitle: Debbie takes delivery of a precious package as a box of abandoned kittens arrives at the clinic.

title: Echo and the Elephants of Amboseli
subTitle: In Amboseli, nine months of unrelenting drought give way to longed-for rains; the elephants have enough to eat; Echo takes the family on a day out to the Snipe River.

title: Echo and the Elephants of Amboseli
subTitle: Male elephants return to the grasslands to do battle with one another for the right to breed.

title: Echo and the Elephants of Amboseli
subTitle: The fight between Vronsky and Gomer has entered its third day as they battle for the right to breed with Dorie.

subTitle: Time-lapse photography reveals the complex world of the sea star; clownfish; whether shark feeding frenzies exist.

title: Blue World
subTitle: Maine lobsters; sucker-equipped fish called remoras stick to sharks and other ocean animals; humans and stingrays.

title: Blue World
subTitle: In Antarctica, Jonathan swims with penguins, dives under an iceberg, finds a jellyfish that measures three-feet across and meets a leopard seal.

title: Adaptable Animals
subTitle: The clock is ticking as Janice has to cast, create, and fit a test brace from scratch; a smooth fox terrier's mysterious issue continues to be a challenge; a post-surgical goldendoodle finds out if his healing is on track.

title: Adaptable Animals
subTitle: After being hit by a truck, an Australian Shepherd comes to a crossroads about if he can walk with a brace on all fours again or have to have an amputation.

title: Adaptable Animals
subTitle: Janice casts a pug for a neck brace and a cat for an orthotic boot; an elderly Shih Tzu hopes to find a simple solution for a possible neurological problem.

title: Super Senses
subTitle: Some animals have crazy looking sense organs, but every feature has a special purpose that has evolved to ensure the survival of the animal.

title: Super Senses
subTitle: Some animals have adapted perfectly to the darkness and can avoid dangers; owls can hear soft sounds from far away; bats orient themselves using echolocation.

title: Super Senses
subTitle: Animals that live underground have developed keen senses to find their way around; wombats have excellent hearing to compensate for their very small eyes; the mole is almost completely blind, but uses its nose to get along.

title: A Dog's Life
subTitle: Two dogs are surrendered to the League's Adoption Return department, while an undernourished mother dog and her five pups are given intensive care.

title: A Dog's Life
subTitle: A stray and her eight puppies are evaluated for adoption, with the League workers hoping that they can rehabilitate the wild mother.

title: A Dog's Life
subTitle: Sadie will need therapy and an extra dose of TLC to overcome the trauma of an abusive owner.

title: A Dog's Life
subTitle: Saving Cuddles, a scruffy terrier mix who was hit by a car and can no longer walk, and Heather, a dog with a behavior problem.

subTitle: Bipin plays the hero after a bungled house raid.

subTitle: The twins' troop crash a birthday party and the city monkey catcher seeks to banish them permanently.

subTitle: A new alpha male is crowned and peace reigns, but the tranquility may not last as Bipin and the twins' troop move closer to the temple walls.

subTitle: Bipin and the twins' troop face off with the Galta Gang.

subTitle: Drawn in by the constant supply of food, urban coyotes cannot resist the sights and smells of Anchorage; highly elusive, no-one knows how many coyotes live in this city.

subTitle: Creatures of Europe: the mongoose; the dragonfly.

subTitle: Steve deals with a bear who was in an inappropriate sleeping spot; a badly wounded bear.

subTitle: The odd behavior of hammerhead sharks; a blue hole adventure in Belize.

subTitle: Whale sharks kept in a giant tank; shark hearing, shark feeding frenzies.

subTitle: Diving with bull sharks in Cancun, Mexico; sharks' electrosensory systems.

subTitle: Anna Breytenbach has dedicated her life to what she calls interspecies communication.

title: Wild Iberia
subTitle: The time of stillness and silence has come, but most animals aren't able to hibernate; many species have had to adapt to this continental way of life.

subTitle: Discovering creatures that have evolved to incorporate spitting and stinging into their deadly arsenal for surviving in the wild.

title: Animal Armoury
subTitle: For these creatures, ejection and odor are potent instruments to capture prey, as well as a means of survival.

subTitle: Examines the lives of the ferocious barracuda, the playful yet aggressive leopard seal and the powerful bull shark.

subTitle: Takes a look at the animal kingdom's most opportunistic eaters and the set of skills they utilize to hunt their prey.

title: Killer Instincts
subTitle: Showcases the smallest yet most dangerous assassins in the animal kingdom and examines how they survive and hunt in the wild.

title: Arctic City
subTitle: One of Alaska's most secretive and efficient predators, Lynx are usually highly elusive; in Anchorage, houses are being built in the Lynx's territory, forcing these usually shy big cats, to find new ways to survive on the streets.
